backward_compatibility.default_behavior_status: document_default_with_chunk_override remains valid; no enum expansion is required by current evidence.

best_schema_variant.selection_reason: D0 remains the minimal contract because the existing three-value schema already reaches 1.0/1.0 when evaluated together with the existing runtime filters.

limitations: This is a post-hoc counterfactual design exercise and does not modify frozen labels or rerun blind authoring.
RET2-015 and RET2-016 remain independent candidate-recall failures and are intentionally out of scope.
The proposal proves satisfiability only when existing candidate metadata is evaluated together with existing runtime filters across the current 40 chunks and 640 case-chunk pairs.
The current evidence therefore supports a protocol/guide clarification before any schema expansion, not immediate metadata versioning.

minimum_required_change.why_minimal: Once the existing runtime filters are joined with the existing three-value mode, all 40 chunks already have at least one perfect assignment.
minimum_required_change.why_not_dual_field: A second field does not improve runtime allow/deny behavior over the existing schema under the current 40x16 counterfactual.
minimum_required_change.why_not_fourth_enum: any_applicable_scope also reaches 1.0/1.0, but it is unnecessary because D0 already does so when evaluated with the full existing runtime join.
minimum_required_change.why_not_guide_only: Guide clarification is exactly the minimal change required; no new metadata field or runtime field is justified by the current evidence.

orthogonal_field_results.conclusion: Scope requirement alone is sufficient for runtime allow/deny. Any second field would be explanatory or QA-only, not required for matching.

values.business_definition: Candidate is reusable when the primary_solution_id is inside the runtime operational scope.
values.confidence_rules: High confidence when the text explicitly centers one primary solution.
values.how_to_distinguish_from_other_values: Use when primary-solution presence is enough and secondary applicable solutions do not independently trigger reuse.
manual_review_conditions: multi_solution wording remains ambiguous after reading the chunk
negative_examples: A compliance prerequisite that should also apply when a secondary applicable solution is the only active one.
positive_examples: An implementation playbook mainly for one solution that still mentions adjacent systems as background context.
values.risk_of_post_hoc_overfitting: Low if the decision is driven by the chunk's own business focus.

values.business_definition: Candidate is reusable when at least one applicable_solution_id is inside the runtime operational scope and excluded_solution_ids do not conflict.
values.confidence_rules: High confidence when the chunk describes a reusable prerequisite or governance requirement for any listed applicable solution.
values.how_to_distinguish_from_other_values: Use when any one applicable solution is enough, even if the primary solution is not the one currently active.
manual_review_conditions: cross_cutting_requirement text mixes shared prerequisite language with explicit joint dependency language
negative_examples: A capability description that is only safe when every listed applicable solution is jointly in scope.
positive_examples: A shared security prerequisite that applies to any listed deployment or controlled-retrieval solution under discussion.
values.risk_of_post_hoc_overfitting: Medium until a new blind authoring attempt independently validates the distinction.

values.business_definition: Candidate is reusable only when all applicable_solution_ids are inside the runtime operational scope and excluded_solution_ids do not conflict.
values.confidence_rules: High confidence when the chunk explicitly couples the listed solutions.
values.how_to_distinguish_from_other_values: Use when partial overlap is not enough and dropping one listed applicable solution would change the business meaning.
manual_review_conditions: the chunk lists multiple solutions but does not clearly say whether they are jointly required
negative_examples: A shared readiness requirement that still matters when only one listed solution is active.
positive_examples: A multi-solution capability note that is only safe when all named solutions are jointly part of the recommendation scope.
values.risk_of_post_hoc_overfitting: Low if the joint dependency is explicit in the chunk content.

values.business_definition: Candidate is reusable independent of operational_solution_scope, subject only to excluded_solution_ids and non-scope runtime filters.
values.confidence_rules: High confidence when the chunk is solution-agnostic policy or process guidance.
values.how_to_distinguish_from_other_values: Use only when the content truly stands on its own across solution scopes.
manual_review_conditions: content looks global but still contains solution-specific boundary language
negative_examples: Any chunk whose meaning depends on a particular solution family being actively discussed.
positive_examples: A global policy statement that applies regardless of which demo solution is currently in scope.

proposed_runtime_schema_changes.notes: Existing operational_solution_scope is sufficient once candidate metadata can express partial applicable-scope reuse.

runtime_joinability.notes: Candidate-only scope matching is not sufficient, and runtime-only filters are not sufficient by themselves. The existing pair of candidate mode plus existing runtime filters is already sufficient under the current 40x16 counterfactual.

applicable_solution_ids: 合规政策RAG检索助手
私有化大模型部署方案
candidates.candidate_semantic_hypothesis: This chunk can remain primary-solution-centered under the current schema, as long as runtime filtering continues to block security_compliance evidence in contexts that do not request that document type.
candidates.citation_label: 受控知识检索安全与合规要求 - 安全要求
candidates.content_semantic_summary: 对于合规政策RAG检索助手和私有化大模型部署方案，必须明确访问控制、日志留痕、引用展示、版本责任人与高风险问题升级路径。若回答涉及制度解释、例外判断或对外承诺，系统必须把人工复核放在流程中。
candidates.generic_business_meaning: A security/compliance evidence chunk centered on controlled-policy retrieval, whose safe visibility depends on both solution scope and existing runtime document-type eligibility.

mode_failure_explanations.full_applicable_scope: Fails because some allow cases only activate one applicable solution, so requiring every applicable solution creates false exclusions.
mode_failure_explanations.global_reusable: Under scope-only analysis it over-allows unrelated cases. Under the full existing runtime join it is also blocked by runtime filters, but that would be an unnecessarily weak authoring choice.
mode_failure_explanations.primary_in_scope: Under scope-only analysis it appears to fail, because some deny cases still contain the primary solution in runtime scope. Under the full existing runtime join, those deny cases are already blocked by document-type filters.

runtime_difference_summary.critical_difference: The deny cases that appear to break primary_in_scope are actually filtered out by existing runtime document-type constraints, not by a missing scope enum.
runtime_difference_summary.gold_observable_pattern: The current benchmark keeps these security chunks only in contexts that explicitly permit security_compliance evidence; the problematic deny cases do not.
